pokemon:araquanid.name: Araquanid
pokemon:araquanid.summary: Despite what its appearance suggests, it cares for others. If it finds vulnerable, weak Pokémon, it protectively brings them into its water bubble.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Despite what its appearance suggests, it cares for others. If it finds vulnerable, weak Pokémon, it protectively brings them into its water bubble.
It acts as a caretaker for Dewpider, putting them inside its bubble and letting them eat any leftover food.
It delivers headbutts with the water bubble on its head. Small Pokémon get sucked into the bubble, where they drown.
It launches water bubbles with its legs, drowning prey within the bubbles. This Pokémon can then take its time to savor its meal.
It has a habit of storing things it values in its water bubble. If its Trainer doesn’t watch out, Araquanid will try to put them in its bubble!
It usually passes its time in the water. When its belly is full, it stores its subdued prey in the water bubble on its head.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Despite what its appearance suggests, it cares for others. If it finds vulnerable, weak Pokémon, it protectively brings them into its water bubble.

pokemon:dewpider.name: Dewpider
pokemon:dewpider.summary: When it comes across enemies or potential prey, this Pokémon smashes its water-bubble-covered head into them.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: When it comes across enemies or potential prey, this Pokémon smashes its water-bubble-covered head into them.
Dewpider normally lives underwater. When it comes onto land in search of food, it takes water with it in the form of a bubble on its head.
It crawls onto the land in search of food. Its water bubble allows it to breathe and protects its soft head.
It forms a water bubble at the rear of its body and then covers its head with it. Meeting another Dewpider means comparing water-bubble sizes.
It can only breathe oxygen that has dissolved in water, so it wears a water bubble on its head when it walks around on land.
When two Dewpider meet, they display their water bubbles to each other. Then the one with the smaller bubble gets out of the other’s way.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: When it comes across enemies or potential prey, this Pokémon smashes its water-bubble-covered head into them.
